channel.title: Homepage on Voylin's Life
channel.description: Recent content in Homepage on Voylin's Life

item.title: Getting Japanese yen in Japan
item.description: JPY in JP Traveling to Japan without any Japanese yen is really not a problem. I remember having a difficult time before I came here for the first time as I could not find a way to get Japanese yen easily in Belgium. The only bank which offered currency exchange from Euro to JPY in Belgium was a bank that you needed to be a member of else you could not use that service.

item.title: My life got busy - Channel/Life update
item.description: It’s been a while A week after my last video I got down with a fever and because my last recorded videos which I hadn’t edited yet turned out to be un-usable … well, I just couldn’t make any videos because of that. Many things have happened in the last couple of months and many more things will be happening in the near future. Stress has taken a very big toll on my body as well, making me less motivated to do anything as every movement is hurting and a struggle.

item.title: I'll take the JLPT this year
item.description: What is the JLPT The Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT) is a test used to evaluate the proficiency of non-native speakers of Japanese. The test is administered by the Japan Foundation and Japan Educational Exchanges and Services. The 5 levels: N1 (The highest level) N2 N3 N4 N5 (The lowest level) The test measures the ability to understand Japanese used in a broad range of contexts and assesses proficiency in grammar, vocabulary, reading comprehension, and listening comprehension.

item.title: Day in my life - Alone with my baby
item.description: Why do I call it babysitting? hahaha I have no idea, it’s my own kid so I should say parenting, but it was one of my first times taking care of the baby completely by myself. It was a fun day and having seen my kid grow up so quickly has been quite amazing. Does he cry a lot? He almost never cries … until I try to make him fall asleep.

item.title: My study routine 2022 - Japanese student
item.description: My study routine My study routine is mainly a morning one as I am a big morning person. I usually wake up at 5 in the morning, have my breakfast, which is always cornflakes, and then I start being productive as having had a productive morning makes me more motivated and happy for the rest of the day. Even when I’m having a busy, difficult and slightly frustrating day at work, knowing that I have achieved some things in the morning can make my overall mood a lot better.

item.title: Nihongo Sou Matome - Review
item.description: What is Nihongo Sou Matome The Nihongo Sou Matome series is a set of books designed specifically for people preparing for the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT). As a fan of these books since I began studying Japanese, I can confidently say that they are an efficient way to study and review for the JLPT. Books per level N5 level: 1 book N4 level: 2 books N3 level: 5 books N2 level: 5 books N1 level: 5 books There are also smaller test books available for each level, which are mainly practice tests.

item.title: All JLPT N2 Grammar - Quick Japanese
item.description: All JLPT N2 Grammar - Quick Japanese A nice “short” video for people studying for the JLPT N2 test ;) In this mini-series, I will help everybody who is going to take the JLPT exams by providing a video with the grammar rules together with an example of easy studying/understanding. Big thanks to my wife and her mother for helping me to make this video. It took a very long time, a little over a month, and on my last day before my paternity leave ends, I was finally able to finish it.

item.title: Do you need a data sim card in Japan?
item.description: Do you need a simcard in Japan? Simple answer? No. If you want more info on why, continue reading. Today I’d like to answer the question “Do I need to buy a data sim card when traveling in japan?” as it is one which not many people ask themselves. Most people just buy a data sim card without thinking about the other FREE possibilities. Nowadays a lot of people like to stay connected with the internet for social media or other things.

item.title: 2 years of living in Japan - Do I want to go home?
item.description: Two years in Japan: a Reflection Introduction Hey guys! For the people who don’t know me, my name is Voylin and I’ve been living in Japan for the past 2 years now. In this article, I’ll be reflecting on my experience so far, sharing what I’ve done, what I like and dislike about Japan, and of course, whether my Japanese has actually improved. Big news First of all, big news: yesterday I went to get my new visa and I finally got three years!

item.title: Beginning of Road to N1
item.description: Road to N1 Road to N1 is the name of the vlog series in which I try to work towards achieving JLPT N1 level. It is the highest level of the JLPT and some say that even Japanese people have a hard time not making any mistakes. It is a big goal and something which is still very far away for me to achieve. Why N1? I have been living in Japan now for some years and my Japanese is still not sufficient enough for everything that goes above having simple conversations.

item.title: 紅葉のおさんぽ
item.description: くもり空が多いこの頃。紅葉の見ごろもそろそろ過ぎてしまうとのことで見に行った。毎年紅葉の時期には行く近くの庭園。夫と歩いたのは初めてだった。季節を一緒に感じられる喜び。一緒にいるからできること。 改めて日本に来てくれてありがとう。

item.title: かにかに
item.description: 10/8は私の祖父母の結婚60周年記念日だった。 このことに2日前に気付いた私の父は何かをしようと企んだが、私が「かに道楽は？」というとそこになった。かに大好き一族。おまけに私の夫もその影響で好きになった。 久しぶりにかにを口にし、懐かしい話を交えながらゆっくり時間を過ごし、特別な日になった。かにの身を取るのは至難の業だ。

item.title: アイスの食べ方
item.description: アイスが残り少なかったので贅沢にそのまま （お下品ですません） アイスの食べ方は実家に住んでいた時とくらベて変わったことの一つ。 大きいすくうタイプのアイスを買うようになり、チョコソースやユニコーンプープ（私たちが勝手に名付けたカラフルな砂糖チョコみたいなん）をかけたり、バナナを入れてバナナスプレッドにしたり。 甘いものを摂取する機会が多くなった、、、ような気がする。 血糖値は今のところ大丈夫。

item.title: All JLPT N3 Grammar - Quick Japanese
item.description: All JLPT N3 Grammar - Quick Japanese A nice “short” little video for people studying for the JLPT N3 test. In this mini-series, I will help everybody who is going to take the JLPT exams by providing a video with the grammar rules together with an example of easy studying/understanding. MISTAKES IN THE VIDEO: 35: The Noun and いAdj conjugation are swapped. – Thanks to reddit user enpitsu89 for pointing this out ;)

item.title: Free Online Guide Through Nara Japan
item.description: Free guides in Nara Japan (online) Want to travel in Japan, but there’s a pandemic going on? Go on an online tour with Nara Student Guides to see a small but beautiful part of Japan. ^-^ Link: https://narastudentguide.org/ I went on on of their normal guides when I visited Japan for the first time and it’s one of the best ways to learn more about Japan and it’s history in a quick, interesting and engaging way whilst strolling through the city of nara.

item.title: Japanese Insect Museum - Closeby Osaka/Kobe
item.description: Insect museum I like many things, electronics, animals, nature, programming, making videos, diy, games and of course insects. I don’t know where my interest for insects came from as my entire family really dislikes them. But I find them very fascinating and interesting. They have such a unique way of living and handling situations. So I was quite happy to go to this insect museum, even though that the people I went with don’t really like insects hahah.

item.title: Quick JLPT Grammar videos - How it's made
item.description: JLPT Grammar videos, how are they made? In this video I explain a bit why these videos take so long to make as many people are requesting the JLPT N2 video to come out already. Some people even ask for an N1 version already, but I wonder how many people know how much work goes into one of these videos. That’s why I made it so hopefully people will appreciate our work even more.

item.title: I'm quitting anki - Why? What now?
item.description: My hate love relationship with Anki I’ve always liked Anki, but the amount of things which you can customize always make it feel like you aren’t learning as efficiently as you may be able to if you would adjust certain things. This makes you waste a lot of time using Anki. This together with over learning and then being stuck with 1000 reviews on a single day can be very de-motivating.

item.title: Glossika review - I can't believe this ...
item.description: Glossika review Now this video got quite a bit of down votes from people who really like Glossika as my review of this service isn’t really great. But many people asked me to review this service and when I review applications/websites to learn Japanese, I really compare quality and price versus other alternatives. The good parts Glossika has a wide range of available languages to choose from which you can learn; Easy and clean interface; Good for sentence mining.

item.title: Pimsleur Japanese - It became affordable??? - [2021]
item.description: Pimsleur Japanese Now that the price is better, I can finally recommend this fully. The Pimsleur language courses are by far the best audio courses which I have found so far. IF you can implement them in your daily routine, this really will become the quickest way for you to learn your target language. The lessons are nicely structured and there is a lot of reviewing as well to keep you from forgetting anything which you’ve learned before.

item.title: My Japanese learning mistakes - Self evaluation 2020
item.description: My Japanese learning mistakes I’ve made many mistakes in the past four years of studying Japanese, in 2 months time it will have been five years since I officially started. I never thought I would continue to study Japanese for this long, but hey, here I am still going … somewhat strong …? It has been quite a journey and I’m not really ashamed to talk about all my mistakes and downs which I had whilst studying this beautiful but complicated language.

item.title: How to start learning Japanese - A beginners guide
item.description: How to start learning Japanese Here’s a beginner guide on how to learn Japan. Because there is no better time to start learning Japanese than right now. You may be in lockdown or you just spend a lot of time at home because everything is closed during this season or you want to stay at home because you want to be safe. This is my advice to beginners who want some kind of a guide to start learning Japanese.

item.title: No JLPT this year
item.description: I am not taking the JLPT this year why? Basically, 2020 has been a disaster, the last thing that needs to happen is that I get sick and spread it to all my 400-500 students. I don’t want to take the test if that would mean that I would infect so many people, but also for my own health. Since moving to Japan I have been sick quite a lot. I don’t know why exactly, maybe because of the food, different living conditions, maybe because Japan wants to make me sick.

item.title: Voylin's wife
item.description: Thank you for visiting our website. I’m Voylin’s wife who will be helping to post articles on this site. I’ d like to share how our life is and share our experiences for the people who are interested or need some information. I am quite a beginner to maintain/post on this kind of website but will try my best … hope you will enjoy it! ^-^ Some explanation by Voylin: This website is made with Hugo, so posting articles and such is not as ‘simple’ as using wordpress by example.

item.title: Ryokan Experience in Japan Nara - Mikasa
item.description: Staying at a Ryokan Staying at a Ryokan is such a nice relaxing experience, although I must admit that the food is always too much and very difficult to eat. But it’s sooooo delicious as well. :D Enjoy the video in which we stay at Ryokan Mikasa in Nara, it really was a very nice stay and had a lot of fun. This was our “honeymoon”, I put it in between brackets as I don’t know as it counts as a honeymoon.

item.title: How to move to Japan - Guide, costs and how to?
item.description: How to move to Japan So moving to Japan can be quite expensive, but how much exactly? In this video, I will talk from the very beginning, all the way until receiving my driver’s license, which was the last step for me. This is a plan that will guide you through all the steps of moving abroad to Japan, in 6 months. First 3 months are the planning and paperwork in your own country, the last 3 months are how to do things in Japan so you can actually start living there without worrying too much.

item.title: Why no videos? - Life update
item.description: Voylin disappeared (again) So my last video was 31 days ago, I would like to explain why I have been so inactive. But I would also like to say that starting today, you can expect a new video from me every single week. ;) I can however not promise that I can keep this up to have weekly uploads, there is work, a wife and a lot of other interests which like to distract me.

item.title: Ultimate Japanese Anki Deck - JPSB
item.description: The last Anki deck you’ll ever need (until JLPT N3) So I have created this deck called JP Study Buddy which can be downloaded at this link: Link Learn this all and you can pass the JLPT N3 test, and you will be halfway passing the N2 as well. ;) But this was made to learn Japanese, not to pass any kind of test, please take that in mind. Took me a week to make it, if you have any suggestions please let me know.

item.title: A day in my life in Japan - During Lockdown
item.description: Day in my life during lockdown in Japan Honestly, not much to do as everything is closed and we are advised to stay inside. Most day’s I study, play games, study and that’s about it. Of course I also eat and sleep, sometimes I even go to work, I know very exciting stuff. ^^

item.title: 38 degrees, hospitals in Japan and life update ^o^
item.description: Being sick in Japan The worst part about being sick is that here in Japan, clinics make you sit in a waiting room for over an hour before it’s finally your turn for your appointment. Yes that’s right, you make an appointment but somehow they’re always late by minimum 40 minutes. I don’t blame the doctors, they are quite overworked probably and a lot of older people really like talking.

item.title: All JLPT N4 Grammar
item.description: All JLPT N4 Grammar - Quick Japanese In this mini-series, I will help everybody who is going to take the JLPT exams by providing a video with the grammar rules together with an example of easy studying/understanding. We found a small mistake in this video, on note 55. It’s supposed to be 続きそうです If you like this video click like if you want to see more subscribe and hit the bell to stay notified of new videos and as always, if you have any questions, please feel free to put them in the comments below.

item.title: Ume Matsuri with friends - Better than Sakura?
item.description: 梅まつり – Ume Matsuri So Ume Matsuri, which basically means “Plum Festival”. It looks similar to Sakura, but never say that to a Japanese person or else you’ll have to hear an hour-long explanation of why it’s different and why Sakura is better ^^” Just sharing a video of me having a fun time with some friends before the heat of the summer nearly killed me a few months after this.

item.title: My first month in Japan
item.description: Visa trouble It has been quite an adventure so far. This whole move to Japan to start living and working here has been kind of difficult, to say the least. That is because of 2 things: Corona and Paperwork. Right now we are almost done with the paperwork. But because of the Corona-virus, the international post has stopped in Europe. The problem is that I still need some official documents from Belgium which need to be sent to me here in Japan.

item.title: Moving to Japan
item.description: Preparing to leave Belgium This whole journey was something I wanted to film as much as possible however, I ended up being way too busy with family, friends, paperwork, … so I couldn’t record as much as I wanted to. Preparing to move out is already a big undertaking, however, I was moving to almost the other side of the world! The packing was troublesome, to say the least and there are still many things still at my parents’ house in Belgium.

item.title: 4 years learning Japanese
item.description: 4 years learning 日本語 It has been 4 years since starting to learn Japanese So time to reflect on all progress made so far and all things that happened while learning (good-bad) So how did I learn it? Let’s start at the beginning of my Japanese language learning journey, for that let’s go back to a couple of weeks before my first trip. I actually had a good friend at that time who knew Japanese quite well.

item.title: Do you need an Electronic Dictionary
item.description: Do you need it? Short answer, probably not. Although it’s a nice thing to have, actually finding use cases for it when you have a smartphone is difficult. Prices for these things have also not gone down at all. If you really want one for whatever reason, try to get them second hand instead, will save you money and you’ll regret it less if you end up not using as much as you thought.

item.title: My Anki Setup – Learning Japanese
item.description: My Anki setup An Anki setup is like a living breathing thing, it always changes and evolves, it basically can grow together with you if you can work with it in harmony. Sadly enough I can’t and I struggle more with Anki and waste more time on customizing everything then I actually spend learning with it. ^^" Well, here’s how my Anki setup looks like at the moment, 1 month before moving to Japan!

item.title: Last family trip before moving - Pairi Daiza
item.description: Pairi Daiza or Paradisio? The park used to be called Paradisio, now it’s Pairi Daisa but I have been coming here since I was small so it’s difficult to change it in my mind when I talk about this park. I like animals a lot and this park also has such nice theming. It really has such a nice and relaxing atmosphere around it that I could easily walk there a whole day and not be tired.

item.title: New year goals for 2020! - No JLPT anymore
item.description: New year goals! Everybody knows new year goals and almost every breaks their new years resolutions. I wonder if I’ll break mine again this year or not. ^^" Luckily I have been starting to make my new years resolutions more and more realistic as I got older. There’s no point in setting goals which you deep down know, you can’t achieve. I’m not saying that you should not aim high, but know what is possible for you to do.

item.title: Lingodeer has a reasonable price for now!
item.description: Lingodeer pricing The pricing of Lingodeer has been a bit all over the place, first free, then donations, then very high prices. Luckily every now and then they have offers (still expensive in my opinion) which makes the price more reasonable. I myself have Lingodeer lifetime and I’m glad I have it … but I haven’t used it after I got it. The Japanese lessons are … great … but not that useful for my level.

item.title: Memrise vs Anki - Which one is better?
item.description: Memrise vs Anki Two different tools, with 2two whole different communities. It is honestly kind of difficult to compare these as it is similar to comparing Ramen and Udon. They kind of look the same, but they are not the same at all. These two tools have been with me ever since I started studying Japanese, one more useful than the other, but they often switch in which one is more useful.

item.title: My Morning Study Routine - Self Studying Japanese
item.description: My morning study routine I’m a real morning person, most day’s I open my eyes at 5 o’clock and I’m fully awake ready to start my day. The only problem I’m facing is my old friend the sofa … He often tempts me to keep sitting there and watching dumb shows which I don’t really care about. ^^" It took me some years to get enough discipline to actually wake up and study in the morning.

item.title: Visiting Kansai in one day - Kansai train pass
item.description: Kansai train pass When you live in Japan, pay close attention to your railway station nearby. They often have day tickets on discount making it possible to see an entire prefecture very easily. I have used these types of tickets a couple of times now and it is so nice as here in Japan, you basically have a train every 10 to 20 minutes. Trains here are so convenient and I love having trips by train as you never struggle to find a parking spot.

item.title: My opinion of 100円 stores + Building a Samurai
item.description: 100 jpy stores They are everywhere in Japan and even though they are convenient, they are not always the cheapest option. If you use a lot of paper and files, try your local supermarket here in Japan if they also have a stationary section. You’ll find prices which are lower or equal, but with better quality. I like browsing through them, but it takes some getting used to before you know if something is a buy or not.

item.title: From Backpacker to ... Suitcaser?
item.description: I hate suitcases I really do, even though it is helpful to have so much packing space, it just feels less convenient and I really dislike the inconvenience which it causes every time when I have to use a suitcase. I prefer a backpack which I can easily carry everywhere. With a backpack I know I have all important things with me, but with a suitcase I always feel like I’m forgetting something.

item.title: How improve your study motivation
item.description: Improving study motivation You know that feeling that you really want to learn a language like Japanese, but you just can’t seem to start learning it. Or maybe you’be been studying, but it’s starting to feel like a job which you’re slowly starting to dislike? Well, that’s probably because your motivation is going by the drain because of something, let’s watch this video to discover how to improve your study motivation!

item.title: All JLPT N5 Grammar - Quick Japanese
item.description: All JLPT N5 Grammar - Quick Japanese A nice small little video for people studying for the JLPT N5 test :3 In this mini-series, I will help everybody who is going to take the JLPT exams by providing a video with the grammar rules together with an example of easy studying/understanding. If you like this video click like if you want to see more subscribe and hit the bell to stay notified of new videos and as always, if you have any questions, please feel free to put them in the comments below.

item.title: My JLPT N2 Study plan
item.description: My JLPT N2 Study plan I know I may not be fully ready yet to take on the JLPT N2, and I may not be ready for it within the next year or 2. But with a good plan and enough dedication, I may be able to achieve this goal soon! Or maybe not, probably not but I’m really trying to stay optimistic whilst staying realistic.

item.title: Japanese Learning Struggles
item.description: Japanese Learning Struggles This language has taken a lot of time and energy out of my life and even after 3 years, where did I get? Practically almost nowhere, every time when I feel I’ve gotten to a point that I could maybe have conversations, I listen to a conversation of real Japanese people and I just get pressed against the ground with 5 tons of reality on me. It’s just soo demotivating to keep studying a language by yourself and not feeling any progression.

item.title: My Japanese Study Books - Self Studying Japanese
item.description: My Japanese Study Books A better title would be ‘My Japanese Self Study Books’ as I am studying Japanese on my own. Studying this language on your own isn’t a fun experience at certain points as trying to find the correct learning materials can be quite time consuming and sometimes takes even more time then actually studying the language. In this video I talk about all the books I use at this point and which books I own.

item.title: How I use Anki for Japanese
item.description: Using Anki for studying Japanese So Anki can be quite a complicated tool and it was quite overwhelming for me when I started using it. I gave up a couple of times before I actually started using it properly as it was just too annoying to deal with all the settings. Ank I gives a lot of freedom and customize ability, but that’s also its weak point as it makes it too complicated to work with.

item.title: Why I stopped using Memrise
item.description: Why I stopped using Memrise Honestly, I have always had a love hate relationship with Memrise, but like in any relationship, you avoid to look at the negatives until they start becoming too painful. I won’t say that Memrise is a bad tool, but it’s a tool which should be used in a certain way and it is not sufficient for my style of studying anymore. I’ll probably go back to Memrise in the future, not certain when but I will at some point.

item.title: First Impression of Japan - My First Day In Japan
item.description: First impression of Japan I could try writing everything down of why Japan was amazing for me and why I could really recommend it and how it changed my life, and so on and so on … But the best way to explain and talk about this is by actually talking. Enjoy the video! ;)

item.title: What to do in Tennoji on a Rainy Day
item.description: Tennoji on a rainy day Tennoji is a vibrant neighborhood in Osaka, known for its shopping, dining, and entertainment options. While it’s a great place to explore on a sunny day, it can be equally as fun to visit on a rainy day. Here are some things to do in Tennoji on a rainy day that will keep you entertained and (hopefully) dry. Museums; Shopping; Onsen/Sento; Harukas; Go to the Harukas building, there are a lot of escalators which you can take up to a certain floor and then end up in a small garden with a nice, free, view of Osaka.

item.title: Secret to Learning Japanese
item.description: The secret of learning Japanese Basically, there is not really any secret, there are some tips I could give you, but that’s about it. If there would really be an easy way to learn this language then most otakus and other people would already be fluent in Japanese. But here are some tips in case you don’t want to watch the video! :p Motivation; Setting realistic goals; Celebrating small wins; Finding meaningful reasons to learn; Surrounding yourself with support (if possible).

item.title: My Japanese Memrise Courses
item.description: My Japanese Memrise courses The thing with Memrise is that it’s very easy to find and add new courses so I’m always searching for different courses which I could give a try. Before I take the JLPT I’ll probably focus myself on the more JLPT vocab and grammar related courses. Another thing I like about memrise is the fast that we can follow people and then you can see in the ranking if you are better then those people, I always try to beat them hahah.

item.title: Typing Japanese on Linux - [AZERY and QWERTY]
item.description: How to type Japanese one linux To be able to type Japanese on Linux you will need Ibus Anthy Use this command and follow the instructions from the video :3 sudo apt-get install ibus-anthy I may update this video in the future with more detailed instructions as Anthy is getting outdated. I would really recommend Mozc nowadays instead. The next part will probably change with the next video, you better just check the Arch wiki as it has a way more detailed page on how to do it and fix certain things when they don’t work!

item.title: How Make a Study Plan
item.description: How to make a study plan In this video, I will show you how you can plan out your study schedule. It is also important to make your study schedule flexible so you enjoy (or at least not hate) it. In this article I’ll try to get a bit more in depth with how to make a study plan. Creating study plans for languages Learning languages, something many people really look up against.

item.title: Pimsleur Japanese Review
item.description: Pimsleur Japanese Review A review about Pimsleur, which I personally find one of the best audio lessons for helping with learning Japanese. But the price tag is unreasonable and hard to justify when there are other audio lessons for free like NHK. NHK AUDIO LESSONS https://www.nhk.or.jp/lesson/english/ This is a nice resource, however I do not support the practice of NHK to act like a mafia in Japan forcing people to pay because they own a television and therefore can wat NHK so they have to pay.

item.title: Full-time Japanese Student (2018)
item.description: Full-Time Japanese Student - A look in my life ^^ In this video, I explain how it is to be studying Japanese in a way like having a full-time job. I explain how it feels like and what you have to take in mind.

item.title: Bellewaerde trip with a friend
item.description: Bellewaerde park I really enjoyed going to this amusement park as a child and I have seen it change so much compared to the first time I was there. Still really enjoy going there and waiting lines are never really that long. If you want some nice attractions and a fun time without too much waiting, go here! Family park This park has always focused itself on families. They have some roller coasters but it’s not their main focus.

item.title: Hiragana & Katakana In One Day
item.description: Hiragana and Katakana in a single day! Let’s learn Hiragana and Katakana today!!! This is the first big step you should take when starting with learning Japanese and once you know them, you don’t have to review often because they are used in all sorts of texts, books and apps for learning Japanese! Site: Tanoshii Japanese I strongly recommend learning Hiragana and Katakana first before starting to learn anything else that has to do with the Japanese language.

item.title: My Japanese Self Study Books - 2017
item.description: My Japanese self study books - 2017 In this article, we will go through some cheap and reasonably priced books for self-study, as well as more expensive options for those who are willing to invest a little more money into their learning journey. Newspapers for Reading Practice One resource that can be quite useful for improving your reading skills is a Japanese newspaper. While it may not be a traditional textbook, being able to read a newspaper will demonstrate a high level of proficiency in the language.

item.title: Plane Convention Fly-in Moorsele
item.description: Fly-In Moorsele Small video of my trip to a plane and fly convention near my city. I’ve always been quite interested in planes and everything that flies (like birds :p). Fly-In Moorsele is an exciting experience for aviation enthusiasts. This annual event, held in the small Belgian town of Moorsele, brings together pilots and aviation fans from all over Belgium to showcase their skills, knowledge and share their love of flying and planes (and everything else which flies with an engine).

item.title: Kayaking Adventure - Semoine
item.description: Kayaking in Semoine A long and hard adventure in a kayak, I felt so tired and I really thought I filmed more. I really like kayaking, but the river was in a very terrible shape with all the greenery. I don’t plan on going Kayaking again soon after this hellish tour through a dense greenery packed river. This was everything except relaxing. This was probably the hardest exercise of my life.

item.title: Lost in a Forest Semoine
item.description: Just a random vlog This is just a random vlog from my time in Semoine when I was there with my family in our mobile home. It was a nice relaxing trip (except for the kayaking in a river filled with greenery). I decided to go for a solo hike and tried to get lost to have some fun. I’ve always liked hiking, but because of the speed at which I walk, my family can’t easily keep up so I go alone.

item.title: Duolingo Japanese for IOS Only??
item.description: Duolingo Japanese only for IOS Update: Duolingo officially got released so this video is kind of out of date … This article will just talk a bit about why IOS users are privileged. So whilst the Japanese course of Duolingo was in beta/alpha, only a selected few people got access to test the course. Of course there were some tricks you could do like changing the url to also get access to the course, same could not be done with the android version of Duolingo.

item.title: W/ Knife through Airport Security
item.description: A dumb story This is a story about probably my dumbest mistake when having gone to an airport. I still remember this as it was quite a big shock and I thought I would have gotten in a lot more trouble then I did. There is really not much to tell about this other then watch the video and have a laugh at my dumb mistake. Morale of the story, always check your pockets if something is still in there, or if there are any holes which may have dropped knives into the inner part of your coat.

item.title: 6 Dog's + Mobile Home Adventure!
item.description: Why a mobile home? We have a big family and for as long as I can remember, we always went camping for our vacations. However, problems started to come when we got more dogs. Traveling with 6 dogs, and 3 kids isn’t the easiest thing. That’s why my parents went over from tents to a mobile home. With my family? At this moment (2022) I think me and my family would probably travel by mobile home as well, just because you carry everything with you and we don’t own a car and our apartment is not big enough to store a lot of camping materials which we may only use once a year.

item.title: Travel List Packing - Essentials Only
item.description: The Importance of Packing Light When it comes to long-term travel, the last thing you want to do is lug around a heavy suitcase. Not only is it a hassle to carry, but it can also limit your mobility. Instead, opt for a backpack that you can easily take with you on any adventure. The Best Way to Pack Your clothes A good place to start packing your backpack is at the bottom.

item.title: Packing List - Electronics - Travel Essentials
item.description: The Essentials: Electronics for Backpacking I am writing this article a lot later after making this video. This article will not be directly attached to this video but may still prove informative. When you’re backpacking, it’s important to pack light and only bring the essentials. This is also true for electronics, as carrying too many gadgets can add unnecessary weight and bulk to your backpack. However, there are some electronic items that are essential for backpacking, and can make your travels much easier and more enjoyable.

item.title: Packing List - Toiletries - Travel Essentials
item.description: The Essentials: Toiletries for Backpacking I am writing this article a lot later after making this video. This article will not be directly attached to this video but may still prove informative. When you’re embarking on a backpacking trip, it’s important to pack light and only bring the essentials. This is especially true when it comes to toiletries, as you’ll need to make sure that you have everything you need to stay clean and healthy, without carrying too much weight.

item.title: Packing list - Clothes -Travel Essentials
item.description: The Essentials: What Clothes You Need While Backpacking I am writing this article a lot later after making this video. This article will not be directly attached to this video but may still prove informative. When it comes to packing for a backpacking trip, it can be tempting to bring along every piece of clothing you own. However, it’s important to remember that space and weight are limited, so it’s important to be selective about the clothes you bring.

item.title: Tree parkour Bali - Last day's in Bali
item.description: My last adventures in Bali My last couple of days in Bali and I kept trying to push my limits, by hiking in a jungle and trying to fight my fear of hights by doing a tree parkour. Those were some of my most exhausting days of my time in Bali. Tree parkour I actually did this with a friend, it was a fun experience. But we were both afraid of heights and could not finish the last parkour.

item.title: Adventures in Bali
item.description: The wonders of Bali I’ll keep this one short and just advise you to look at the video. I had an amazing time and forced myself more and more to get out of my comfort zone. I even rented a scooter and made my way through the busy traffic there. Looking back now, the things which I did on that scooter were really irresponsible and I am glad I am still alive.

item.title: Arriving in Bali Indonesia - Second country of the Big Adventure Trip
item.description: My arrival in Bali When I arrived in Bali I was happy, but also very exhausted. I arrived and basically slept in the airport because I booked my hostel for the wrong day. Still not certain how I managed to do that, but it happened. I also felt quite sad for some reason, looking back now I realized that having stayed in Japan for over a month made it feel quite like home and I started feeling too comfortable being there.

item.title: Last days in Japan
item.description: Leaving Japan Leaving Japan was difficult and I was going to go to Bali next. But before even arriving in Bali I had already booked my next plane ticket to go back to Japan. Something (actually more someone) was pulling me back there. That person was also the reason why I went 8 times in total to Japan with my 9th time being to move here. Why leave if I was going directly back to Japan?

item.title: A stroll through Osaka and Nara light festival
item.description: Nara Light festival A yearly festival which is really worth going to. It basically feels like you are entering Disneyland but then without the attractions. I had an amazing time there with the people I’ve met in the hostel there in Nara and the life lessons they gave me. Often in these videos about my Big Adventure, I don’t really show or say why I was actually on that trip. It was to sort out my own life a little bit.

item.title: Nara Adventure - Mount Wakakusa
item.description: A hike in Nara One of the first missions I wanted to achieve was hiking in Nara. Probably a bad idea to do this during summer as the mosquitos were trying to eat me alive and I really did not bring enough water at all. It was quite an experience and a good punch to start good on this big adventure to to test my limits and wake myself up from the slumber in which my mind had been the past years.

item.title: Departure on My Big Adventure
item.description: My big adventure Still one of the most life changing trips which I have taken in my life, as I quit my job to go on this adventure. What many people, including my family, didn’t know was that I had a mission, a goal for this trip. People thought I just wanted to travel, but there were more reasons behind this.

item.title: 7kg Backpack Worldtrip - Winter edition
item.description: In the video I showed how I prepared my bag to travel long-term in winter. It is colder so you need sweaters and warm clothes, those things can take up a lot of space. Still, I managed to keep the overall weight down to 7KG. I show how I did this in the video. But why would you want to keep your backpack so light? Convenience and Mobility; (Carrying only one bag makes it possible to go to different places and to travel faster compared to with a suitcase) Cost Savings; (No paying needed for luggage when you have carry-on luggage) Simplicity and Minimalism; (Keep your life simple and mess free by carrying only necessary stuff) Improved Safety.

item.title: Travel Clothes - Fabrics and fast drying
item.description: In this video I’ll be testing different types of fabric to see which is the best for traveling as a backpacker, enjoy! ;)

item.title: My Room - Belgium
item.description: My old room in Belgium Since I moved out to live in Japan (2020), I have been missing my room as it was the place where I started with this youtube channel. I’ve had many fun, sad, depressing, excited and relaxing feelings in that room. We were living in that house since my 3 year of Junior high. Often the complaint came from me that the room was too small, but I was always able to make it work.

item.title: Streetfestival Belgium
item.description: What is a street festival? In my neighborhood in Belgium where I used to live, we started having a meetup once a year to improve the overall relationship between the neighbors as our whole area had only 1 entry/exit street. This is one of our festivals which I recorded and wanted to share with you guys, enjoy! Is this common? No idea if this kind of a thing is common in Belgium, especially nowadays.

item.title: Check Your Blood - Travel advice
item.description: Why is checking your blood important for traveling? In the video I talk about some reasons, but in this article I want to expand a little upon that. It’s not only important to check your blood before traveling, it is something which should be checked regularly. Once a year is advised by doctors. Here in Japan (where I live now) we need a medical check once a year, which is probably like this in most countries.

item.title: First trip to Japan
item.description: Departure to Japan February 2016, my first trip to Japan!! I was so excited to finally go to this country, but what made me decide to go to this country is something I haven’t told many people about yet. I was actually planning on going with a friend, but those plans got canceled soon after I bought my plane ticket. Kind of disappointed but as I’m no stranger to solo traveling I decided to still go instead of canceling my plane ticket.

item.title: Travel Stress
item.description: Travel stress for solo travel First time traveling completely solo to Japan. Here is a playlist of all videos of this solo trip and the blog post: My playlist! My blog post! Before big adventures I always get stressed and start thinking about all the ways certain things can go wrong to prepare myself for them. But especially when traveling to foreign countries where you haven’t been to before can really cause some worries.

item.title: Best Plane Seats
item.description: Plane seats Probably some of the most uncomfortable things about having a trip to a different country by plane. In this video we go over some of the tips on how to make this more comfortable. The video has some advice, and in this article I would like to talk a bit more about that and about some other things to consider. If you’re planning a trip on an airplane, you may be wondering where to sit to get the most comfortable and enjoyable experience.

item.title: Bad Weather Vlog
item.description: Am I an artist now? Vlog 3 - Bad weather = being creative I’ve got bored of staying inside because of the bad weather so I decided to work more on the drawing on my door. Of course there are multiple other things you can do when it’s rainy weather, I just chose to be creative, here are some other things which you may consider doing: Watch a movie or TV show; Read a book or listen to an audio-book; Play board games or video games; Do a puzzle or craft project; Write in a journal or blog; Bake or cook a meal or dessert; Practice an instrument or sing; Do yoga or exercise at home; Take a relaxing bath or shower; Have a picnic indoors; Visit a museum or art gallery; Go to an indoor play area like Round 1; Have a game night with friends or family; Attend a class or workshop on a topic you’re interested in; Go shopping or browse stores; Visit a local library or bookstore; Go to a coffee shop or cafe; Take a nap or get some extra sleep; Do some indoor gardening or plant care; Take a virtual tour of a place you’d like to visit; Watch youtube videos of Voylin’s Life.

item.title: 4 Most Common Travel Scams
item.description: Most common travel scams Whilst traveling, there’s nothing worse than falling into a scam and possibly losing a lot of money, ruining your vacation or in the worst case, getting stuck abroad because you have nothing left to afford your way back home nor contact home. In this video I’ll be talking about some popular scams and how to avoid them, enjoy! ;) Taxi’s So in the video, I talked about the concept of “broken taxi meters” and whilst this is the most common one that has to do with taxis, there are a couple of other things to watch out for as well.

item.title: Budgeting whilst backpacking
item.description: Traveling is expensive Yes, traveling is more expensive than sitting at home doing nothing. But it doesn’t have to cost an arm and a leg. There are so many possibilities nowadays to travel cheaply and still have an awesome experience. Of course, it can’t be free, but you can, however, buy yourself a tent, decent backpack and some basic tools you will need to go camping by example, then your main cost will only be food if you walk everywhere and avoid transportation.

item.title: Japan - Travel advice 2
item.description: Drinking in public Although it is allowed to drink out in public, you may want to think twice about drinking in Japan as the prices can be a lot higher than where you come from. Especially beer is kind of expensive for what you get as Japanese beer isn’t as strong as others. Drinking at an Izakaya, Japanese bar, or restaurant can easily cost you 500 jpy a beer, which is around 5 dollars.

item.title: Starting With Photography
item.description: Starting with photography Today I wanted to try out photography, to see how good or how bad I am. xp I had these pictures saved somewhere but I lost them sadly enough. ^^" Oh wel, look on my instagram if you want to see if my skills became any better hahah. I’ve had to learn quite a few things and I am still learning today on how to improve my pictures.

item.title: One Bag Travel - Winter
item.description: Backpacking during winter When thinking about backpacking, most people would think about Thailand, Bali, India, and so on … But a lot of backpackers also go traveling during winter, even better, some even take stuff with them for summer AND winter inside of a carry on backpack!! It’s strange to look back to it now but I aspired to be one of those people someday, I got married and started living abroad now.

item.title: Sleeping on a plane
item.description: Choosing the best seat for sleeping The best seat to sleep is a window seat, not only for the reason of being able to put your head against the wall to sleep in a somewhat more comfortable way but also because you can’t get interrupted by people wanting to pass by you to go to the toilet. Only downside is, if you wake up and really need to go to the toilet you will have to bother 2 people.

item.title: IC Cards
item.description: What are they? They are rechargeable cards which can be used to pay for trains, busses, some vending machines and even in some shops. From my own experience, I can confirm that it’s possible to pay in most convenient shops with them. They are prepaid and require no pin code, which makes it dangerous to load up a lot of money on it, because if you lose it, your money is gone as well.

item.title: Japan - Travel advice 1
item.description: Transport The best way to move yourself around in Japan is with the trains and an Icoca pass, the pass changes names in certain places. What this pass is is a card which you load money on and can then be used to pay in almost all trains, busses and even certain shops allow you to pay with it. There’s only one problem, lose the card and you lose your money.

item.title: What you can't take on a plane
item.description: So you are about to go on a trip but you are not sure what exactly you can take on the plane with you, well look no further as I have made a handy video talking about this subject! Or you can just read the list below. :p Special Requirements for ‘Carry-On’ luggage: Lithium Batteries Large lithium batteries need to be put in a non-electrostatic bag and contacts must be covered by non-conducting tape.

item.title: Planning a trip tips - Backpacking tips
item.description: Planning your upcoming trip can be difficult, there are many things to keep in mind. Plan your trip too much and you take all the fun and adventure away, plan too little and end up sleeping on the streets as everything is fully booked. Or spend a lot more money then you thought and come back home with debt. Everything is possible! :D Here are 5 things which you should keep in mind when planning your trip!

item.title: Posting a Letter - Day-trip by Bike
item.description: A day-trip by bike This is the first video I really put the effort into making. But I find that the contrast between the time that I made this video (now 6 years ago) and now, is quite big ^^" Posting a Christmas letter the special, and personal way :D It was a fun day-trip and now 6 years after making this video, I feel kind of nostalgic about Belgium and the relaxing area where I used to grew up in.

item.title: Goals in Life (2015 - Start of my youtube channel)
item.description: Goals in life So I am revisiting this old video in December 2022. I won’t change the date of this article and instead update this every now and then. You can see what my goals were in 2015 when watching the video, and on this page you can see how my goals have changed. December 2022 It is december 2022, the 9th the be exact. I am living in Japan now, I’m married and I have a kid, all things which I did not expect to happen at the making of this video.

item.title: Blog
item.description: Welcome to my blog! Here I post all sorts of articles, mainly articles about the videos I make. I make videos/articles about learning Japanese, Life in Japan, Travel advice, … I hope you enjoy your stay! ;) Blog Categories: Learn japanese  (31) Our life  (16) Travel advice  (29) Voylins life  (96) Show all articles Some recent articles: Getting Japanese yen in Japan  2023-02-04  -    Voylin's Life , Travel Advice Traveling to Japan without any Japanese yen is really not a problem.

item.title: Contact us
item.description: We can not promise that we will reply within a day, nor a week. We have a busy life full of adventures. But we will reply if needed and we will try our best to not let you wait too long. Loading…

item.title: Who we are
item.description: Voylin’s Life Hey guy’s, my name is Voylin, I come from Belgium and I’m living in Japan with my family. I started the youtube channel Voylin’s Life to share my travel experiences and advice at first, however I became in love with Japan, and with someone in Japan, which made me change the focus of this channel to my life in Japan, things about Japan and helping others to learn Japanese.
